description_long: This object is a classic flintlock or percussion cap pistol, characterized by its curved wooden grip and long, octagonal metal barrel. The frame and barrel are made of dark, possibly blued or aged steel, while the handle is crafted from polished wood with a rich brown hue. The trigger guard is prominent and metallic, and the hammer mechanism is visible above the grip, indicating a single-shot, muzzle-loading design. The barrel is relatively thick and features two parallel bores, suggesting a double-barreled configuration. There are no visible modern attachments such as rails or optics, and the overall design is reminiscent of 18th or early 19th-century firearms. The finish is slightly glossy, with some wear visible on the wooden and metal surfaces.
description: A double-barreled antique pistol with a curved wooden grip and a long, dark metal barrel, featuring a prominent hammer and trigger guard.
description_view_0: Side view showing the curved wooden grip, long double-barreled metal barrel, and exposed hammer mechanism.
description_view_1: Front view highlighting the two parallel barrel openings and the symmetrical alignment of the hammer and trigger guard.
description_view_2: Opposite side view displaying the same features as view 0, with the grip and barrel profile clearly visible.
description_view_3: Rear view focusing on the rounded end of the wooden grip and the back of the hammer assembly.
